englishDefinitions: A medical condition caused by overheating, characterized by heavy sweating, weakness, rapid pulse, and dizziness.
A heat-related illness less severe than heatstroke, but still requiring immediate treatment.
examples: Several runners suffered from heat exhaustion during the marathon on a very hot day.
Symptoms of heat exhaustion include heavy sweating, pale skin, and muscle cramps.
If you feel heat exhaustion coming on, move to a cool place and drink plenty of water.
